STATEMENT OF WORK
PROJECT: NGIS PHASE 3 — DATA CENTRE CONSOLIDATION
Document Number: SOW-NGIS-P3-2024-001
Version: 2.1
Date: September 15, 2024
Contracting Authority: Shared Services Canada (SSC)
Classification: Protected B

---

1. BACKGROUND AND CONTEXT

Shared Services Canada (SSC) is undertaking the Next-Generation
Infrastructure Strategy (NGIS) Phase 3, which involves the consolidation
of three Government of Canada (GC) data centre facilities into a single
modernized enterprise data centre. This initiative supports the GC Data
Centre Strategy and aligns with the Treasury Board Policy on Service and
Digital.

The three facilities in scope are:
- Site A: Gatineau, QC (primary consolidation target)
- Site B: Ottawa, ON (secondary consolidation target)
- Site C: Hull, QC (tertiary facility)

---

2. SCOPE OF WORK

2.1 IN SCOPE

The following activities and deliverables are within scope for this
Statement of Work:

a) Data Centre Consolidation — Physical
   - Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure
   - Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A
   - Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A
   - Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization
     at Site A

b) Network Equipment Migration
   - Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches
     across all three sites
   - Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones
   - BGP routing table migration and validation
   - Network performance baseline testing post-migration

c) Active Directory (AD) Migration
   - Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers
     to consolidated infrastructure at Site A
   - AD schema extension and forest restructuring
   - Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing
   - Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD

d) Project Management and Reporting
   - Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office
   - Steering committee presentations (monthly)
   - Risk register maintenance
   - Change management and stakeholder communications

2.2 OUT OF SCOPE

The following activities are explicitly excluded from this Statement
of Work:

a) End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile
   devices) — this is addressed under a separate procurement vehicle
   (NGIS Phase 4)

b) Application modernization or re-platforming — any changes to
   application code, architecture, or hosting platform are out of scope.
   Applications will be migrated as-is (lift and shift only).

c) GC-Cloud workload migration — migration of any workloads to public
   cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) is out of scope and is
   addressed under the GC-Cloud Wave 4 project.

d) End-user training and change management beyond project communications
   — departmental training programs for end users are the responsibility
   of client departments.

e) Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes
   resulting from platform migration are out of scope.

f) Network monitoring tool upgrades — upgrades to the network operations
   centre (NOC) monitoring platform are deferred to a separate initiative.

---

3. DELIVERABLES

The Contractor shall provide the following deliverables:

D-001: Project Management Plan — Due: October 31, 2024
D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due: December 15, 2024
D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due: January 31, 2025
D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due: February 28, 2025
D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due: March 10, 2026
D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due: April 30, 2026
D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report — Due: May 31, 2026
D-008: Project Closure Report — Due: June 30, 2026

---

4. CONSTRAINTS

4.1 Budget
The total approved budget for NGIS Phase 3 is $2,400,000 (two million
four hundred thousand dollars), inclusive of all fees, disbursements,
and applicable taxes. Any expenditure beyond this amount requires a
formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure
and submission to Treasury Board if the variance exceeds 15%.

4.2 Timeline
The project must achieve Go-Live (D-006) no later than April 30, 2026.
The final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year
2025–2026). Extensions beyond this date require ADM-level approval.

4.3 Regulatory and Policy Compliance
All work must comply with:
- ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach
- Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy
- Treasury Board Policy on Government Security
- Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)
- Official Languages Act (for all client-facing deliverables)

4.4 Change Control
Any changes to scope, schedule, or budget must follow the SSC Project
Change Management Process. Scope changes require written approval from
the Project Sponsor and must be assessed for impact on cost and schedule
before implementation.

---

5. PERFORMANCE STANDARDS

- System uptime during migration windows: minimum 99.5%
- Data integrity: zero data loss during migration
- Rollback capability: all migrations must include tested rollback procedures
- Security: all activities must maintain Protected B data classification
  throughout

---

6. PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE

Start Date: October 1, 2024
End Date: June 30, 2026

---

Document Control:
Prepared by: SSC Contracting and Procurement
Approved by: Director General, IT Infrastructure, SSC
